title: Samsung Galaxy C9 Pro Review – Worth Your Money?
content:
Supplementing Samsung’s smartphone line-up, the Samsung Galaxy C9 Pro has now entered the market offering the latest specs with few surprises. For people who love big screen smartphones, C9 pro is a treat. Samsung has packed a 6-inch screen coupled up with a powerful octa-core processor from Qualcomm, 6GB of RAM and a huge 4000mAh battery. The Samsung Galaxy C9 Pro is a solid phone, without any doubt, and has most qualities of a high-end flagship phone. However, is it really worth the price tag of Rs.60,000 in Nepal? Let’s find out in our review.

**Related in Reviews:**  
[Gionee A1 Full Review: A true mid-range phone to be reckoned?](https://www.enepsters.com/2017/05/gionee-a1-review/) | [Xiaomi Redmi 4A Full Review – The best budget smartphone in Nepal?](https://www.enepsters.com/2017/03/xiaomi-redmi-4a-full-review/)

### Design

In the time of our review, we really appreciated the unibody build of the phone that made the C9 Pro more premium and sturdy. The meticulously built smartphone feels huge while you hold it in the hands, courtesy of its humongous display. Albeit the fact that the phone houses appreciable metal body, the phone basically resembles every other phone in the market.

The bezels around the screen are very thin and help to minimize the phone’s size. Despite having a 6-inch display, the phone isn’t too big to hold with one hand, however, it may depend on the size of your hand. But for most of us, the ‘too big’ phone shouldn’t be a worrying factor. The phone has a power button on the right side with one microSD card tray and thankfully a separate dual-sim tray. On the left, it has the volume rockers and the buttons feel premium and clicky, while there’s also a fingerprint sensor embedded right into the home button.

### Display and Audio

Design aside, the crowned jewel of the C9 Pro tends to be its Super AMOLED display. The phone has one of the biggest display in the market in such a light form. The 6-inch 1080p Super AMOLED display on this phone offers clear and punchy picture quality — an excellent companion for those media junkies. And then there’s the added Corning Gorilla Glass protection for those clumsy users out there. The screen can get really bright thus, bearing no problem during daytime visibility, and also gets fairly dim for night usage. The sensors on the phone do a great job of adjusting the screen brightness throughout the day.

C9 Pro’s audio, on the other hand, is a different matter. The phone has one speaker grill on the bottom right side that gives loud sounds with low-base, though it comes with a ‘Stereo Mode’ that plays sound via the main speaker as well as the earpiece.

### Camera

While the Samsung’s S7 and S7 edge had the best camera in the Android market, C9 Pro falls behind those most flagships out there. This doesn’t mean that C9 Pro doesn’t capture great images. The pictures we took from this phone had great details and exposed just a tad more than necessary. The Galaxy C9 Pro has a 16MP main shooter with f/1.9 aperture that takes crisp, sharp and color-accurate pictures. During low-light situations, the phone struggles to focus and processes the images too much that washes out small details.

At the front, C9 Pro has similar 16MP selfie shooter with f/1.9 aperture. Directly competing with the camera phones out there in the market, the front camera is as good as it gets. Besides the camera’s focus, the selfies taken from the camera puts subject in focus with good lighting. Even when the subject is in shadow, the software processes to produce a decent selfie.

Back Camera Sample

Back Camera Sample

Back Camera Sample

Front Camera Sample

### Software

C9 Pro comes with Android 6.0.1 Marshmallow with Samsung’s new Grace UX on top. Some basic but handy functionalities like a Blue light filter, data saving mode, S-Power planning are added into the OS. Then, there’s Samsung’s “Always On” display option that lets you show the time and date with notification icons in white with the background. This doesn’t consume much battery, all thanks to its AMOLED display.

The big size of the phone sometimes presents a challenge while using the phone, mostly for people with small hands. So, there’s the One Hand mode for easy usage that shrinks the whole screen. Although the phone doesn’t have Nougat yet, it features multi-window applications and pop-up application modes that allow using two or more apps at once without minimizing the other.

### Performance and Battery

With a powerful octa-core processor, C9 Pro handles heavy gaming and multi-tasking well. The phone is powered by a Qualcomm Snapdragon 653 SoC along with an Adreno 510 GPU. The benchmarks put C9 Pro’s overall CPU performance head-to-head with S7, so it is safe to say that this phone offers good processing power.

Memory wise the phone is backed up by 64GB expandable internal memory and has a whacking 6GB of RAM — the first of its kind from Samsung. While playing high-end games like Dream League Soccer and Asphalt, the phone didn’t lag once, which was expected. The ample amount of RAM easily handled multiple apps on the background without any stutters.

The phone, though, lags somewhat on battery management. Despite housing the battery friendly AMOLED display, the 4000mAh battery on this phone lasts through a full day with an hour of gaming and watching movies and other light tasks like internet browsing and phone calls. It would have been better if there was a flagship or battery efficient chipset from Samsung to handle battery management well on this phone, so as to extend battery life even further.

### Verdict

It’s a safe bet.

I am not saying it’s the best smartphone in the market, but it is packed with great hardware specs and a good enough camera. After Note 7’s debacle, C9 Pro is here to fill the void before the S8 is here. I got fond of the big display after a while, and although the phone isn’t powered by the high-end Qualcomm processors, the octa-core processors handled every task I threw at it smoothly. I wish Samsung did better, however, I can’t complain much about this phone since it’s targeted to a niche market(those who prefer design over top specs).

So after being all cleared, what do you think about the new Galaxy C9 Pro? Is it worth the money? Will you get one for yourself? Let us know about your thoughts in the comment box below.

title: Coolpad Cool 1 Review – Best mid range phone with dual-camera?
content:
Coolpad Cool 1 is one of few dual-camera smartphones at a mid-range price of around 30,000. The phone comes with a capable Snapdragon processor and an immersive display.

**Related in Reviews:**  
[Xiaomi Redmi 4A Full Review – The best budget smartphone in Nepal?](https://www.enepsters.com/2017/03/xiaomi-redmi-4a-full-review/) | [Gionee A1 hands on and first impression](https://www.enepsters.com/2017/03/gionee-a1-hands-first-impression/)

Dual cameras are all the rage these days. If one camera is not enough, let’s put in a two camera set up for better pictures, right? Although the dual cameras look like a gimmick at first, the new tech trend has gained a momentum and is able to offer great camera performance. But, do you need a dual camera phone and is Coolpad Cool 1 the one you should get? Let’s find out in our review.

### Design and Build

Cool 1 has an aluminum unibody design curved at the sides. The slight curves make the phone easier to hold. At top and bottom, the phone has polycarbonate material that adjoins the whole body. The phone has volume control buttons and power buttons on the right side. There’s a slight camera bump on the back and a polished fingerprint sensor. The phone has an average build and we have seen better body and designs at the price range.

### Display

Coolpad Cool 1 comes with an impressive 5.5-inch IPS LCD display offering Full-HD(1080p) resolution and 403ppi pixel density. The phone is bright enough to watch on direct sunlight and offers dynamic movie and gaming experience. Plus, the phone’s display saturation can be adjusted from the settings to suit your needs as well. There are not many complaints about the display, besides the glaring thick bezels around the screen.

### Performance

Cool 1 has some downsides but it covers them very well with its excellent performance and processing power. It boasts a Qualcomm Snapdragon 652 octa-core CPU along with an Adreno 510 GPU. The phone handles daily activities smoothly, while gaming in this phone is a breeze as well. The phone can easily run graphic-intensive games like Asphalt and Sniper 3D without any lags. The phone only heats up slightly around the camera after half an hour of gaming but cools down when left for a minute. Coolpad has also included a 4GB RAM in Cool 1 that makes this phone efficient while running multiple apps at the same time.

### Camera

Cool 1 has a dual-camera setup at the rear and an 8MP front camera with a wide-angle lens. The phone does capture good selfies and takes better photos in low-light as well. The camera software doesn’t do many touch-ups(beautify the image) and for me, that’s a good thing.

The dual-camera has a 13MP RGB(color) sensor and a monochrome(black/white) sensor on the back. Together, they produce fine images with good contrast and exposure. The saturation is a bit off in auto mode but can be adjusted in Pro mode to produce perfect shots. The images have more noise and lack enough detail.

Camera Sample

Camera Sample

Camera Sample

Captured using DSLR Mode

Captured using DSLR Mode

SLR mode in Cool 1 offers DSLR-like the feature of blurring out the background to focus on the subject — to be exact, like a bokeh effect. The ‘SLR mode’ thus captures decent portraits. There’s a neat feature for SLR mode images, where you can adjust the intensity of blur for these bokeh pictures.

The phone can record up to 4k videos and offer other neat features like Slo-Mo video rec, Pro-mode, Beauty mode and much more.

### Software

Coolpad Cool 1 runs on Android 6.0 Marshmallow with EUI skin on top. The phone lacks an app drawer and drop-down quick settings. The quick-settings can be accessed by long-pressing the menu button. There’s a one-handed mode for using the big phone with one hand easily. It is a bit irritating that the running app is paused whenever quick settings are accessed to turn wifi off or access other options.

### Battery

The phone also has a big battery of 4060mAh capacity that supports Quick Charge 2.0. The phone has the new USB Type-C port instead of the common USB-2.0 port. The new Type-C USB offers faster charging and data transfers and is the future. We welcome this change in this price range and encourage other brands to do the same. The phone easily lasts a day even with heavy gaming and media consumption. On daily use, the phone lasted me a solid 32hours of screen-on time.

### Verdict

Coolpad Cool1 offers powerful performance and dual-cameras along with a large battery. The phone is definitely a good buy if you like a powerful phone. Design and build are alright, whereas the cameras couldn’t be better than this.  
The phone gives you a powerful android experience with beefy specs and should appeal to power users. Coolpad Cool 1 has a lot to offer, as a great performing phone with an average camera and the battery life just makes the deal more appealing. All in all, the Coolpad Cool 1 pretty much justifies the 30,000 price tag and without hesitant, this is one of the best mid-range smartphones with dual-camera.

title: Gionee A1 hands on and first impression
content:
Gionee A1, the latest smartphone from Gionee, is set to launch in Nepali this March. In the next weeks after March 15, the phone will be available on the market at the disposal of Nepali consumers. Until then, here’s our hands-on experience of Gionee A1 that packs Gionee’s best for the android lovers.

**Related in Reviews:**  
[Coolpad Cool 1 Review – Best mid range phone with dual-camera?](https://www.enepsters.com/2017/03/coolpad-cool-1-review/) | [CAN Infotech 2017 impressions – Smartphones and laptops](https://www.enepsters.com/2017/01/can-infotech-2017-impressions/)

The phone feels good in the hand and offers the same market standard aluminum body with rounded corners. At first glance, you might mistake Gionee A1 with Oppo phones or even iPhones, but there’s more to it than meets the eye. Gionee has built upon its previous phone line-ups and packed the best of both Marathon series and selfie series in Gionee A1. The phone comes with a 16MP selfie camera and a 13MP back camera, along with a 4010 mAh battery. In our brief interlude with Gionee A1, we tried the updated camera app and the selfie camera which is on par with the best phones in the market. The main 13MP camera isn’t as impressive as the selfie camera but should perform alright. We will test the cameras in future reviews once we get the review units.

The 18W dual core fast charging from Gionee is a new feature touch up from Gionee. The already huge battery will now charge quickly as well. Gionee promises no thermal problem with safer charging but we have obvious concerns after recent battery problems in many flagships. At first look, the phone seems to have a fingerprint sensor on the back right where it has the Gionee logo, but the fingerprint scanner is embedded in the home button at front.

Gionee A1 also sports an Octa-core 2.0GHz MediaTek processor with 4GB RAM and 64GB ROM. The 5.5″ Full-HD AMOLED display provides better colors and contrast compared to other IPS LCD screens. Overall, the phone looks decent and might as well justify its price in Nepal and India.

The prices of Gionee A1 has not been fixed for Nepal and India yet. Internationally, it is priced at $349, so we expect the price of Gionee A1 to be NRs. 35,000 in Nepal and INR. 20,000 in India.

title: Best Camera Phones in Nepal
content:
Taking photos means a lot these days. As our interactions are mostly limited to the internet, photos offer our conversations and interactions more meaning. The visual delights that photos give are astounding and the moments captured are hard to forget. As the phones have advanced throughout recent years, they got some major camera upgrades. Here we have a list of best camera phones in Nepal for taking amazing photos.

**Related in Buying Guide:**  
[Tucano Backpacks now available in Nepal](https://www.enepsters.com/2017/03/tucano-backpacks-price-in-nepal/) | [Repair your broken iPhone screen at LCD Refurb Lab Nepal](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/12/repair-iphone-screens-lcd-refurb-lab-nepal/)

Phones these days rival with compact digital cameras and even DSLRs to deliver outstanding and detailed photos. As the saying goes, the best camera is the one that’s with you. So let us give you a list of best camera phones in the market right now.

Please note that the phones are listed according to their prices.

# Camera Phones in Nepal

### iPhone 7 Plus (Nrs. 113,550)

[iPhone 7 Plus](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/10/iphone-7-7-plus-price-nepal/) houses a 12MP camera sensor on the back. The new camera has a bigger f/1.8 aperture, a distinct upgrade from the f/2.2 sensor from iPhone 6s. Apple finally included an Optical Image Stabilisation(OIS). As a result, the camera performs better than its predecessor. Usually, people manage to take great pictures with iPhones because of their own camera software which provides noticeable image processing. A bonus feature for iPhone 7 Plus is its telephoto lens in addition to the 12MP wide-angle lens. The 12MP telephoto lens allows 2x optical zoom(not to be confused with digital zoom) and works great with portrait shots. It’s a 58mm f/2.8 camera lens that’s coupled with the primary camera sensor supports the zoom feature.

At the front, iPhone 7 Plus has a 7MP camera with a f/2.2 aperture. All these numbers and specs amount to a great photography experience. The phone can take good low-light images, thanks to it’s bigger camera sensor and wider -aperture along with the OIS. And if that’s not enough, there’s the quad LED flash on the back to lighten objects.

The premium handset supports 4k video recording @30fps and records 1080p full HD videos at a maximum 120fps. The OIS works great with the videos providing smooth videos.

### Samsung Galaxy S8 (Nrs. 88,900)

[Samsung Galaxy S8](https://www.enepsters.com/2017/04/samsung-galaxy-s8-s8-plus-price-nepal/)[Plus] not only boasts an appealing design but it also houses the best camera from Samsung right now(probably one of the best in the market). On the back, it has a 12MP camera with a 1/2.5″ sensor size and f/1.7 aperture. The phone snaps great photos with its primary camera and works well in low-light conditions. The phone features PDAF(phase detection autofocus), OIS, and LED flash. The autofocus is also better than most because of dual-pixel tech. As a whole, the camera on the phone justifies its title as one of the best in the market and offers detailed and crispy photos.

The phone has an upgraded 8MP front camera with a 1.4 µm pixel size and f/1.7 aperture. Taking selfies from S8 will now considerably produce great images and has enough details as opposed to its predecessor. It performs as good as any front camera on a smartphone can. The phone also supports 4K video recording at 60fps for high-res videos, if you’re into it, along with 1080p recording option at a maximum 60fps.

### Sony Xperia X (Nrs. 60,000)

Sony pioneers in smartphone camera sensor technology. Many major smartphones like OnePlus, Google Pixel, Xiaomi Mi5s and even Apple uses camera sensors from Sony. Sony smartphones, thus, have an upper hand here. [Xperia X](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/08/sony-xperia-x-series-nepal/), the latest flagship phone from Sony, lives up to the reputation. It has a 1/2.3″ 24 MP main camera with a f/2.0 lens. The phone snaps photos quickly with enough details and impressive quality. The huge camera sensor is capable of taking photos with Hybrid autofocus. This means you can track a moving subject without losing the focus on your subject.

Up front, Sony has packed a 13MP camera with f/2.0 aperture. For a selfie camera, the image taken by this phone is on par with the best ones in the market. The phone has a premium build but comes with a mid-range processor, so the camera on Xperia X makes it a phone worth its price.

### Huawei P10 Plus (Nrs. 75,000)

[Huawei P10 Plus](https://www.enepsters.com/2017/06/huawei-p10-plus-price-nepal/) comes with a dual 20MP + 12MP camera setup on the back. There’s a normal 12MP color sensor coupled with a 20MP monochrome(black&white) sensor destined to produce crisp images. The camera is once again co-designed with the famous camera company, Leica and carries amazing image-taking capability. It can absorb more light through its monochrome sensor, which works even in less than sustainable lighting conditions. The dual cameras work together to give deep contrast and saturated image. Combined with its hybrid autofocus, P10 Plus’ cameras are the best from Huawei, period.

### OnePlus 5 (Nrs. 59,499)

Despite carrying a low price tag, the phone from OnePlus goes head to head against the aforementioned heavyweights. The [OnePlus 5](https://www.enepsters.com/2017/07/oneplus-5-price-nepal/) is the successor to last year’s OnePlus 3 and 3T and boasts improved specs in every possible way. The handset sports a dual 16MP(f/1.7) + 20MP(f/2.6) camera at back with plethoras of features including PDAF, Electronic Image Stabilization(EIS), Optical Image Stabilization(OIS) and LED flash.

Up front, for selfies, the phone carries a 16MP camera(f/2.0) with PDAF and EIS. Aside from those camera specs, the phone comes with Snapdragon 825 chipset, 6GB of RAM, and 64GB of onboard storage, all in all with an aggressive and impressive price tag.

### Mi 5s (Nrs. 50,000)

Xiaomi has already launched [Mi 5s](https://www.enepsters.com/2017/01/xiaomi-mi-5s-available-pre-orders-nepal/) and Mi 5s Plus in Nepal. Mi 5s, the successor of Mi 5, packs a 12MP Sony 378 sensor with 1.55 µm pixel density, same camera sensor as from Google Pixel. Thus, the phone takes detailed and well-lit photos. Image taken from Mi 5s under perfect conditions have high clarity and details. In short, if you are looking for a phone with a good camera and build with the latest hardware, Mi 5s is it. For selfies, the phone packs a 4MP camera with f/2.0 aperture rate.

### Coolpad Cool1 (Nrs. 30,996)

Coolpad, the latest mobile brand in Nepal, offers dual-camera smartphones at a mid-range price. [Coolpad Cool1](https://www.enepsters.com/2017/02/coolpad-debuts-4-new-smartphones-nepal/) comes with the 13MP dual-camera setup. Similar to the P10 Plus, it has one color camera coupled with a monochrome camera. The phone offers decent camera performance at the price range. We will comment more on the dual camera when we get to review the phone. On the spec sheet, the camera looks solid. Plus, the 8MP front camera is icing on the cake for those selfie fanatics.

### Samsung Galaxy J7 Prime (Nrs. 29,490)

Being a mid-range phone from Samsung, the [J7 Prime](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/09/samsung-galaxy-j7-prime-nepal/) is people’s favorite for sure. The J series is the most popular Samsung phone series in Nepal and the J7 Prime proves why. The handset’s camera offers nothing fancy but it gets the job done for most of the people. The smartphone also lacks any gimmicks such as a dual camera or else but offers the best combo of features and quality. J7 Prime has a 13MP main camera with f/1.9 aperture that snaps images with just right saturation and detail. At the front, there’s an 8MP camera which takes way better selfies than older models like J7.

So, what do you think about the aforementioned phones? Have we missed any eye-catcher? Let us know in the comment box below. We will keep updating this list of the best camera phones in Nepal. Follow us for more updates.

title: Samsung C9 Pro hands-on at Samsung Blogger’s Meet
content:
Samsung C9 Pro, the ‘Super Phone’ as quoted by Samsung Nepal, comes with a bunch of eye-catching features. The phone was available for pre-booking and on display at the [CAN Infotech 2017](https://www.enepsters.com/2017/01/can-infotech-2017-impressions/). Carrying a price tag of Rs. 60,000, the highlight of the phone remains to be 6GB of RAM paired up with a set of 16MP rear and front camera.

**Related in News:**  
[InFocus set to launch M812i in Nepal](https://www.enepsters.com/2017/02/infocus-launch-m812i-rumor/) | [Meizu M5s officially launched with 3GB RAM and 13MP camera](https://www.enepsters.com/2017/02/meizu-m5s-officially-launched/)

The phone offers a neat build and a premium body. At first, C9 Pro looks ridiculously big for a phone and likely unfit for normal pockets. But, the big phone carry slim profile and an impressive Super AMOLED display with an awesome color reproduction. In our brief hands-on, the screen looked bright and clear — an absolute treat for media junkies.

As for the primary camera, the 16MP tag for the phone seems exaggerated, to tell the truth. However, the 16MP selfie camera seems good and lives up to the expectation. But, in our limited time, the few camera snaps we managed to take lacked details. We expect to see better camera performance in our detailed review.

Rest aside, the Samsung C9 Pro carries varieties of perks, including an appealing metal body and a Super AMOLED display. Though it remains to be seen if it actually is better than the phones already on the market with similar specs and prices. Wait for our detailed review of C9 Pro to learn more.

title: Xiaomi Mi Mix set to launch before the end of February in Nepal
content:
Xiaomi Mi Mix, the gorgeous bezel-less phone from the Chinese company took over the world by storm. The phone features top-notch specs and has on of the most stylish and elegant design. The phone was a big [surprise](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/10/xiaomi-mi-mix-unveiled-bezel-free-smartphone/) to most of the tech enthusiast, thanks to its promising big bezel-less 6-inch display, which, by the way, is the main highlight of the phone.

**Related in News:**  
[Nepal Telecom brings 4G service for its prepaid users](https://www.enepsters.com/2017/02/nepal-telecom-brings-4g-service-prepaid-users/) | [Honor 8 Lite goes official with 3GB of RAM and Android 7.0 Nougat](https://www.enepsters.com/2017/02/honor-8-lite-goes-official/)

Vatsal Impex, the authorized distributor of Xiaomi phones in Nepal is set to launch the Xiaomi Mi Mix until(or before) the end of February. I got the chance to use the phone today at Blogger’s meet of Mi Nepal and honestly, it was an awe-inspiring experience. The phone comes with a Snapdragon 821 Processor along with a 6GB of RAM.

Besides that, the phone has a few modifications on front camera position and the speaker. To manage a bezel-less display, the front camera is moved to the lower-right corner of the phone. Plus, the phone has a piezo-electric actuator as the main speaker. I truly admire the new tech that resulted the clean and elegant display of Mi Mix.

To sum it up, the ‘concept’ Xiaomi phone that should pass as a functional phone is expected to price at flagship range. As is the trend with Xiaomi in Nepal, the phone will definitely be priced higher than the international market. The company, however, hasn’t fixed the price yet.

title: CAN Infotech 2017 impressions – Smartphones and laptops
content:
CAN Infotech(or CAN Infotech 2017), the annual event held by CAN(Computer’s Association of Nepal), comes with many promises. For some, it arrives as the time to upgrade their gadgets by switching to a more updated smartphone or shopping for a better performing PCs or graphic card. The allurance of the CAN Infotech is the promise of discounted products and the ability to browse through a long list of electronic components and services at a single location. As for me, it’s the time to get myself updated with the latest gadget offerings and arrivals.

**Related in Reviews:**  
[Gionee A1 hands on and first impression](https://www.enepsters.com/2017/03/gionee-a1-hands-first-impression/) | [New Road Mobile Mahotsab Review](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/12/new-road-mobile-mahotsab-review/)

Major distributors and resellers envy this event to advertise their new products and to clear out the old ones. I was underwhelmed while going through CAN Infotech 2017 but still managed to find out new products that struck my fancy.

### MSI GE62 Gaming Laptop

The spec sheet alone notifies of its extreme power. The new VR-capable gaming laptop from MSI has arrived in Nepal with a price tag of Nrs.2,14,000(minus CAN discounts). The laptop boasts the new GTX 3GB 1060 Graphic Card along with a 6th Gen Intel i7 processor.

### Samsung C9 Pro

Samsung has introduced a new smartphone in CAN this year with flagship class specs. Priced at Rs. 59,990 the phone has a bigger 6-inch Super AMOLED 1080p screen and a huge 6GB RAM. Besides these oddly big changes, the phone has a mid-range Qualcomm Snapdragon 653 processor from the 600 chipset series. The chipset from the 600 series has been previously used in the most acclaimed Redmi Note 3 from Xiaomi.

The phone has a 16MP primary camera with a f/1.9 aperture, PDAF focus tech and can shoot up to 1080p videos. At the front, there’s a 16MP selfie camera. The phone should provide highly detailed selfies with its high pixel numbers and even rival the likes of Oppo camera smartphones. With a bigger screen comes a bigger battery, the Samsung C9 Pro houses a 4000mAh battery thus ensuring enough power for a full day use. The phone isn’t yet available on the market but is expected to be launched until the end of February.

### Xiaomi Mi Mix

The new concept phone from Xiaomi, [Mi Mix](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/10/xiaomi-mi-mix-unveiled-bezel-free-smartphone/) has finally arrived at Nepal. The phone hasn’t yet been launched but is on display at the Xiaomi’s stall(or a sealed box, to be exact). To sum the phone up, it comes with a bezel-less 6.4-inch display offering an unorthodox design with a selfie camera at the bottom. There are many changes in the full design of the phone. In the end, Xiaomi has produced a phone with a powerful Snapdragon 821 2.35 GHz processor, 4GB RAM, and a beautiful design.

### OnePlus 3T?

We were expecting the launch of OnePlus 3T at the CAN Infotech this year. However, we only got to see the OnePlus 3 and OnePlus 2 at display in CAN. The phone is expected to launch in coming weeks until then OnePlus 3 is the best OnePlus has to offer in Nepal.

Beside these new gadgets, the CAN Infotech was the hub for many smartphone brands and Internet service providers like Vianet, Subisu, WorldLink, etc offering ‘large’ discounts. Some resellers have priced smartphone and laptop accessories less than usual at CAN, thus attracting hordes of people. Besides that, CAN Infotech makes for a fun visit to a place with loads of pamphlets and gadgets in the display.

Stay tuned for more news on CAN Infotech.

title: CG 65-inch 4K TV officially launched in Nepal
content:
On the occasion of the 10th anniversary of Chaudhary Group, the company launched a total of four products yesterday at the CG Partner’s Meet. Along with the new [CG Blaze 4G phone](https://www.enepsters.com/2017/01/cg-blaze-4g-officially-launched-nepal/), the company announced its new LED TV lineup featuring its first CG Ultra High Definition 65-inch 4K TV. In an attempt to compete with the already established TV brands like LG, Sony, and Samsung, the CG 4K TV is unbelievably priced at Rs. 157,990 in Nepal.

**Related in News:**  
[Samsung Galaxy S8 – All new leaks and rumors](https://www.enepsters.com/2017/01/samsung-galaxy-s8-leaks/) | [CG Blaze 4G officially launched in Nepal](https://www.enepsters.com/2017/01/cg-blaze-4g-officially-launched-nepal/)

Considering the competition, CG looks to provide us with the most basic features for a 4K TV. It comes with a large 65-inch 4K A+ Grade Achromatic Panel, unlike the IPS LCD panels generally offered by other brands. The TV comes with Android 4.4 KitKat OS along with app store compatibility.

CG has embedded a 1.5 GHz dual-core CPU and a quad-core GPU into the TV for better graphics and online playbacks. There’s a 1GB of DDR RAM and a total of 4GB internal memory on the TV. For sound enhancement, the TV comes with a Special Box Speaker, said CG.

CG also launched its Trolley Speaker and Chest Freezer at the launch event. The Trolley Speaker has a front LCD panel that sets it apart from other general speakers whereas the discreetly named ‘Silver Colored Hard Top Door Freezer’ comes with a compressor operatable at a low 160V power supply.

Although CG has made a solid bet by entering the TV market in Nepal, the international brands are already people’s choice for a large high definition 4K TV, and it’s still too early to tell if the CG’s own TV can lure the people.

title: Samsung Galaxy C9 Pro launch details in Nepal
content:
Samsung Nepal is launching its one of the latest handsets in Nepal, Samsung Galaxy C9 Pro. The phone ticks all the boxes in the spec sheet for a decent smartphone but is also expected to cost accordingly. (Update: Samsung Galaxy C9 Pro is priced at Rs. 59,990 in Nepal).

**Related in News:**  
[Colors launches Pride P32 in Nepal](https://www.enepsters.com/2017/01/colors-launches-pride-p-32-in-nepal/) | [Samsung Galaxy J2 Ace officially unveiled](https://www.enepsters.com/2017/01/samsung-galaxy-j2-ace/)

The phone sports a 6.0-inch Super AMOLED display with a resolution of 1080 x 1920 pixels. Samsung Galaxy C9 Pro is powered by a Snapdragon 653 octa-core processor and Adreno 510 GPU. Talking about the memory and storage, the device is paired up with hefty 6GB of RAM and 64GB of onboard storage which, if not enough is expandable via a microSD up to 256GB.

The whole phone runs on Android 6.0.1 Marshmallow on top of Samsung’s own Grace UX. C9 Pro packs a non-removable 4000mAh battery inside and is accompanied by a fingerprint scanner in front. The C9 Pro also comes with a USB Type-C port mostly adopted by the flagships phone these days.

The phone is a treat in case of the camera. Samsung C9 Pro boasts a 16 MP front camera with a f/1.9 aperture along with a 16MP primary camera with PDAF(Phase Detection Auto Focus).

Samsung Galaxy C9 Pro is expected to be launch until the end of February and should probably be priced above Nrs.50,000. The phone leaves almost no stones unturned to satisfy users, the question though remains if the phone lures the users already bedazzled by the likes of OnePlus 3 and Samsung’s own flagship phones.

title: Xiaomi Redmi 4A officially launched in Nepal
content:
Xiaomi Nepal has officially launched the Redmi 4A phone in Nepal for the price of Rs. 14,999. The phone comes with the most basic features available in a budget phone in the market right now. The phone will be available in Gold and Rose Gold color options at [official website](http://xiaominepal.com.np/booking/) and retailers.

**Related in News:**  
[Huawei P8 Lite(2017) officially launched](https://www.enepsters.com/2017/01/huawei-p8-lite-2017-officially-launched/) | [Lava A3 officially launched in Nepal with fingerprint scanner](https://www.enepsters.com/2017/01/lava-a3-officially-launched-nepal/)

*Update: Xiaomi Redmi 4A is now available for Rs. 12,999 in Nepal.*

The phone has a 5-inch HD(720 x 1280 pixels) IPS LCD display and a 1.4GHz Snapdragon 425 processor as its powerhouse. Redmi 4A sports a 2GB RAM and 16GB onboard storage expandable up to 128GB. Plus, the phone is backed up by a 3120 mAh battery which should provide larger on-screen time. So, the battery size is a plus factor for Redmi 4A compared to its competitors.

On paper, the phone has an average camera. The phone uses a 13MP main camera with LED flash alongside a 5MP front snapper. The phone runs on Android 6.0.1 Marshmallow with MIUI skin on top and supports 4G LTE connectivity.

With the arrival of Redmi 4A, fans of Xiaomi can now choose to buy a budget phone with the latest available specs and larger battery life. The phone was recently launched in November 2016 so we really appreciate the quick launch and hope to see the company following the similar footstep in coming days.

title: Nokia 6 launched in China – First Android Nokia phone
content:
Nokia has finally launched an Android smartphone dubbed as Nokia 6. The phone comes at a mid-range price point and offers a nice metal build, with added audio support for audiophiles.

**Related in News:**  
[Lava A3 officially launched in Nepal with fingerprint scanner](https://www.enepsters.com/2017/01/lava-a3-officially-launched-nepal/) | [Intex Q7n Pro officially launched in Nepal](https://www.enepsters.com/2017/01/intex-q7n-pro-officially-launched-nepal/)

To go into details, Nokia 6 has a 5.5 inch 1920×1080 Full HD display with 2.5D Gorilla Glass. It has an aluminum uni-body build providing “superior craftsmanship and design quality” in the words of Nokia. The phone comes with a 4GB RAM and 64GB internal storage along with the Qualcomm Snapdragon 430 SoC. On the camera side, it has a 16MP main camera with Phase Detection Auto Focus and an 8MP front camera.

The company has also included dual amplifiers and Dolby Atmos support to deliver better audio performance. The phone has a 3000mAh non-removable battery and supports dual sim cards as well. Nokia 6 comes with Android 7.0 Nougat OS providing the user support for all the apps and games that come with the Android experience.

The Lumia series from Nokia and the phones with Windows OS was a disaster. Nokia 6 is the start of a new generation of Nokia phones which can jump start the Nokia brand to deliver much better smartphones. For now, Nokia 6 is the only phone from Nokia and looks great in this mid-tier segment. The hardware side is a bit under-powered, but the phone must be worth the money by the looks of it.

Unfortunately, [HMD Global](http://www.hmdglobal.com/press/2017-01-08-nokia-6/) doesn’t have any plans to release Nokia 6 in the global market. The phone is priced at 1699 Yuan($245) and will be available only in China through JD.com.

title: New Road Mobile Mahotsab Review
content:
New Road Mobile Mahotsab sounded like a good expo or event to buy a phone. There was the promise of high discounts on purchase of mobile phones for interested users. However, the fair, which began from yesterday turned out to be a letdown of sorts(for us). Let us discuss what you can see on this fair and why it’s uninteresting.

**Related in Reviews:**  
[CAN Infotech 2017 impressions – Smartphones and laptops](https://www.enepsters.com/2017/01/can-infotech-2017-impressions/) | [Colors Pride P85+ Review – The best camera on a budget phone](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/12/colors-pride-p85-review-nepal/)

### Leagoo and ZOPO Phones

The fairly new smartphones brand, Leagoo, and ZOPO were covering the Pako streets where you can try out their phones and decide for yourself if they are good or not. There were phones ranging from Rs. 10,000-40,000 from these brands, so instead of always buying a Samsung you could try out if the Leagoo and ZOPO are worth your money or not.

### Ncell Free SIM Cards On Smartphone Purchase

Ncell was offering free SIM cards to the shoppers at the fair. The SIM cards will have free data of around 20MB on it, but does it really matter? A free SIM card is okay but is it really that big of an offer for people spending thousands on a phone.

### Gift Hampers and Coupons

Going to Newroad Mobile Fair is not fun. Walking down the overly crowded Pako street is a struggle on its own, but it’s all worth it if you get gifts on every phone purchase, right? The customers are sure to get gifts at this fair if it’s any consolation. What kind of gifts? That depends on your purchase. Most retailers were offering phone accessories like selfie stick or powerbanks on the purchase of specific smartphones.

### Mascots

Who doesn’t like mascots? There were mascots from companies like Gionee and Leagoo, ready to lure in most of the consumers.

### Final Words and some details on discounts

This time Newroad Mobile Fair is not really worth it. I must encourage you to go Pako for the fair if you are in search of a new phone to browse through different options since, all major brands like Samsung, Huawei, Oppo, Gionee were showcasing their phones at one place. However, if you are expecting heavy price cut or discounts, you will get a discount of roughly 2-3k or around 5 to 10% discount on any smartphone.

We give a thumbs up to the organizers for pulling this off and presenting varieties of smartphone brands under one periphery but we would love to see a more organized fair the next time with better offers and highlights.

title: Colors Pride P85+ Review – The best camera on a budget phone
content:
Colors Mobile recently launched two new phones in Nepal, namely [P85](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/11/colors-pride-p85-nepal/) and P85+. Both phones are priced low and host useful specs required in a budget phone these days. Shifting focus to the Colors Pride P85+, the phone is an impressive piece of tech with a good value for money carrying a price tag of Rs. 12,500 in Nepal. The phone has an attractive design, an HD display and a pair of cameras capturing much better photos than other phones at this price range. Is the phone really great option to buy on a budget or just a disappointment? Well, let’s find it in our review.

**Related in Reviews:**  
[New Road Mobile Mahotsab Review](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/12/new-road-mobile-mahotsab-review/) | [ZTE Blade V7 Lite Review – A well built budget smartphone](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/10/zte-blade-v7-lite-review/)

### Design

P85+ has a plastic body with a curve at the sides joining the front and back. The phone feels sturdy while holding and provides a good grip. The phone lacks any capacitive buttons and instead provides on-screen buttons. The handset aware users about notification with a LED light under the screen placed at the center. The phone features a single speaker grill at the bottom, thus when placed on a flat surface, the speaker doesn’t mute out.  The phone comes with a removable back from where you can add two SIM cards and a microSD card.

### Display

Colors Pride P85+ packs a 5.3-inch HD(320ppi pixel density) IPS Oncell display with a layer of Asahi-enhanced glass protection. The display is all too well for general multimedia consumption and casual gaming and gives enough brightness for daytime consumption. There seems to be less screen available while browsing through web or typing due to the on-screen buttons on this phone, though the buttons automatically hide when not in use. Other than that, this phone’s display should hit home for most people.

### Performance

The Colors Pride P85+ is powered by Mediatek 1.3GHz MT6580 quad-core processor along with Mali-400 GPU. The phone has a 2GB of RAM and 16GB of expandable internal storage. Due to the software, the phone leaves very low RAM space but still manages to offer good multitasking support with about 3-4 apps running in the background without any problem.

### Camera

On the paper, the P85+ features a 13MP back camera which takes somewhat average photos with some focus problems. At the front, the phone has an 8MP camera to take selfies with very good results. The selfies taken from the front camera have very good details and decent exposure. The selfies aren’t too washed out like from the cameras of low pixel counts and give true-to-life colors. However, the camera does struggle to maintain focus, mostly in a group photo which tends to blur the subjects farther from the camera.

The camera interface is also modified in this phone with basic options like HDR, Panorama, etc. The photos taken with HDR tend to be better in low-light conditions, whereas, at well-lit scenes, the 13MP camera over-exposed the objects with HDR mode on. Despite these minor setbacks, we wouldn’t hesitate to recommend this phone’s camera to anyone on a budget. Below are some images captured from the device.

### Software

P85+ runs on Android 6.0, Marshmallow with some minor tweaks from Colors. The phone has a stock android feel to it but comes with a set of custom icons. The icons for Gallery, Music, Messaging apps have been added to the phone. Plus, the camera app also features some changes in its interface. Colors have added a bunch of extra apps like SastoDeal, WhatsApp, Colors app, Traffic Police, etc into the phone by default.

### Battery

With this much features on the side, the phone comes with a 2600mAh Li-ion removable battery giving a screen on time of about 4-5 hours on daily usage. The phone can easily last a day of usage with basic web browsing, media consumption, and mild gaming sessions.

### Conclusion

The Colors Pride P85+ is not the best phone in the market, clearly surpassed by the mid-range tiers from Samsung and Huawei. If you can spend more than 20k, you would get a much feature rich phone with a powerful processor and extra advantages, but at a price of Rs. 12,500, we really can’t complain. Despite the low outcome, the P85+ still makes a great phone with its large screen, 8MP front camera, near to stock Android, and a good-looking design.

With several phones from Colors delivering less than expected, the P85+ seems like an exception, thus it should please most of the users. Colors have delivered this time, and I must agree that the phone is worth the price.

title: Repair your broken iPhone screen at LCD Refurb Lab Nepal
content:
Smartphones and iPhones, in general, are now much more prone to accidents more than ever. With constant usage and unintentional carelessness, many consumers are facing the problem of broken smartphone screens. The touch screens are sensitive components of phones and therefore should be kept safe. Most people do that by taking precautions while using phones or by applying tempered glass over the touch screens.

**Related in Buying Guide:**  
[Best Camera Phones in Nepal](https://www.enepsters.com/2017/02/best-camera-phones-in-nepal/) | [When and which Android phones are getting Android 7.0 Nougat update](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/11/when-and-which-phone-android-7-0/)

However, people don’t baby their smartphones forever, thus ending with a damaged screen slapped alongside the pricey bills for repairing and replacing the screens. This hits the pockets of iPhone users harder than the Android crowd with the expensive repair rates for iPhone screens in the market. If an iPhone user were to drop their iPhone 6s and break the screen, he would have to lose about Rs. 27,000 for replacing it with a new screen.

Fortunately, there’s a new option in town for quick and affordable screen repairs. LCD Refurb Lab Nepal, a fairly new entrant in the market, has started to offer screen repairs for iPhones(no Android repairs for now) with the same quality and assurance as from the official repair centers. At first, the repair rates look too good to be true.

#### Repair Rates at LCD Refurb Lab:

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Model Name** | **Glass Replacement** | **LCD Replacement** |

| iPhone 6 | Rs. 2,500 | Rs. 4,000 |

But, many manufacturers have availed close replicas for iPhones with little to no difference in the quality. All repair centers use the same materials for repairing iPhones and so does LCD Refurb Lab, so there should not be any problem with the repairs. title: Yantra 5.0 – Art, Science and Technology Fair kicks off from today
content:
Robotics Association Of Nepal(RAN) started Yantra as a national robotics competition to gather up people interested in tech and robot making and engaging them for improving the state of robotics in Nepal. The winners of Yantra were sent to Indian Institute of Technology for participating in International Robotics Competition. Yantra 5.0 is also organized for the same purpose but with an additional motive of including non-techies like artists, doctors, educators, engineers, and students in the robotic revolution.

**Related in Best Of Us:**  
[Take a look at five ongoing mobile technology trends](https://www.enepsters.com/2017/03/take-look-five-ongoing-mobile-technology-trends/) | [Phones that were cool before smartphone even existed](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/08/phones-cool-smartphone-even-existed/)

**Related in News:**  
[Nepal Telecom brings day data pack offer](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/12/nepal-telecom-brings-day-data-pack-offer/) | [InFocus M680 officially unveiled in Nepal with 13MP front camera](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/11/infocus-m680-price-nepal/)

Yantra 5.0 is organizing several events for a week starting from November 30(today) which includes the Robotic Competition, Creative Computation, Art-Tech Exhibition, etc.

> The mission is to create platform that aims to encourage interdisciplinary cross-pollination among the creative and technical fields with a focus on learning and exchange. Each Edition of Yantra features Robotics competitions, workshops, art-tech exhibition and other multidisciplinary programs. Theme of the Yantra 5.0 is “Agro-Innovation”.

Yantra 5.0 is specifically focused on integrating the Robotics tech into agriculture for easing the work of Nepali farmers. Hence, Yantra 5.0 with the theme ***Agro-Innovation*** is organizing ***International Robotics Competitions*** for tech enthusiast, ***Creative Computing*** for transferring the skill of coding, creating and computing to artists, doctors, and bankers. There’s also the ***Art-tech exhibition*** to explore the work of artists and engineers, ***Paper  Presentation*** review development of the existing products and ***Yantra Learning*** Software competition for a software developer. Yantra is also hosting a ***Yantra Incubation*** to encourage, curate and mentor the innovation into a viable business focused on Agriculture and ***Yantra Kids*** for pushing young ones into tech and robotics. Here’s what’s gonna happen in these events as RAN attempts to grow the maker culture in Nepal.

### International Robotics Competition

#### 1.Krishak Copter

Krishak Copter is the main event of International Robotics competition. The main objective of the event is to design and develop a quadcopter that can spray pesticides in the field. The quadcopter is meant to ease farmers of Nepal, thus is named Krishak copter.

#### 2.Yantra Racer

In Yantra Racer, participants with age no more than 20 compete to showcase basic robotics knowledge. Participants have to build an automatic white line tracking robot later to be controlled manually through an app and race their respective innovation for winning. The robot should travel on a race track with slopes and obstacles to win the race.

#### 3.Yantra Akhada

In Yantra Akhada, robots have to push each other out of the game arena to win, much like the traditional sumo wrestling. There’s a Manual Akhada for robots controlled by the participants and an Automatic Akhada where two autonomous robots have to push each other out of the arena.

### Art and Tech Exhibition

Art and Tech Exhibition aims to create a collaboration of engineers and artists to assist disabled farmers by creating works that enhance their livelihood and income.

### Paper Presentation

Many scholars are researching in the field of robotics and electronics automation all around the world. There are many efficient machines and robots that are yet to be implemented in industrial devices. This event is focused on encouraging such researchers and scholars working in agriculture to share their research with potential investors and entrepreneurs.

### Yantra Learning

Yantra Learning is one of the new program added this year under the umbrella of Yantra for Agricultural disaster response. Yantra Learning is a software competition for programmers to develop software projects that can make the devices learn from its environment by analyzing data and patterns to take decisions . In Yantra, Learning programmers have to develop algorithms to predict and alert about probable natural disasters to save farmers from any climatic damage.

### Creative Computing

Computing and robotics have been in reach of techies and engineers only for too long, though it can be used in other sectors. Creative computing enables professionals from fields other than tech to utilize the benefits of automation and robotics in their respective expertise. Artists, doctors, bankers and entrepreneurs all can benefit from this approach and make technology accessible to people in general. Professional educators and makers would be conducting this session to encourage artists, doctors, and bankers to use technology for the effective execution of the tasks they are performing to explore the impact of technology in their day to day activities.

### Yantra Kids

Yantra is also set to organize Yantra Kids for involving children in the tech sector by conducting workshops and challenges. Children will participate in design challenges after going through a series of workshops and training on topics like design thinking, prototyping, tinkering with knowledge of basic electronics and coding.

### Yantra Incubation

Yantra is organizing competitions for robot makers and rewarding them with cash prizes for some time now. Though, there has not been any path for such winners to use their expertise until now. This time, Yantra is organizing the Yantra Incubation to give a chance to the competition winners for working on solutions to the native problems in agriculture.

**“The focus area would be the identification of the feasible solution to the existing Agro- problem, developing a prototype, business model development and market analysis, mass product development and a tech start-up. At least 3 projects will go through Yantra Incubation for next 12 months.”**

For more information on Yantra 5.0, click [here.](http://www.ran.org.np/yantra5-0/)

title: LEAGOO smartphones officially lands in Nepal
content:
[As rumored](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/11/leagoo-smartphones-land-nepali-market-soon/) the Chinese smartphone brand LEAGOO has entered in Nepali market. Being fairly new and less recognized in the global market, LEAGOO has upped their game by introducing smartphones with better features at a comfortable price. The company has introduced a whole range of devices in Nepal with varying price range and features including a fingerprint scanner, auto-focus cameras, and screen protection.

**Related in News:**  
[Intex Aqua 4.5 3G officially unveiled in Nepal](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/11/intex-aqua-4-5-3g-nepal/) | [OPPO A37 receives a price cut in Nepal](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/11/oppo-a37-receives-a-price-cut-nepal/)

Currently, JSS Group  is responsible for sales and distribution of LEAGOO smartphones in Nepal and has released phones of 4 different series ranging from cheap feature phones to mid-range and high-end devices.

### Z Series

The feature phones with low-end configuration fall into this series with a minimum price of Rs. 5,700. Z series is for budget-minded users with no particular expectations from their phones except making calls and some low-end smartphone experience.

* **Z1** —**Rs. 5,700** : *4-inch FWVGA Display, 1.3GHz Quad Core Processor, 512MB RAM, 8GB ROM, Camera (3MP and 2MP), 1400mAh Battery, GPS, Bluetooth, FM, WiFi, 3G network*
* **Z3** —**Rs. 6,150** : *4.5-inch, IPS LCD Display,  1.3GHz Quad Core Processor, 512MB RAM, 8GB ROM (32GB expandable), Camera (2MP and 2MP), Battery 1800mAh, GPS, Bluetooth, FM, WiFi, 2G, 3G network dual sim*
* **Z5** —**Rs. 7,199** : *5-inch FWVGA, 1GB RAM, 8GB ROM (32GB expandable), 1.3GHz Quad Core Processor, Camera (5MP w/ flash and 2MP), Battery 2300mAh, GPS, Bluetooth, FM, WiFi, WCDMA/GSM network*
* **Z5 LTE (4G)** — **Rs. 9,999** : Same as Z5 with additional 4G support.

### Shark Series

There’s only one phone in this series, namely Shark 1 Lite offering high battery capacity. The phone offers a 5000 mAh battery and costs Rs. 13,799. It sports a 5-inch HD IPS LCD display with 1 GB RAM and 8GB internal storage(expandable to 32 GB). The phone is powered by a 1.3 GHz quad-core MediaTek processor and packs a 13 MP back camera and 8 MP selfie-snapper. The behemoth battery brings a better screen-on time and also serves as a power bank of sorts to charge other phones.

### M Series

The M Series from the company brings durability including the shock proof displays and batteries. M5, one of the three phones, has a 5-inch IPS LCD HD display and costs Rs. 11,999 in Nepal. The phone has a similar 1.3 GHz quad-core processor and packs an 8MP rear camera and 5MP selfie camera. Surprisingly, the phone also features a fingerprint sensor which simply sweetens the deal. In terms of memory, the phone is backed up by 2GB RAM and 16GB internal storage. The whole phone drags the power from a 2300 mAh battery.

Other two phones in this series are M8 and M5+ which have larger screens and cost NRs. 15,499 and NRs. 16,599 respectively. M8 has a 5.7-inch display and fast charging for it’s 3340 mAh battery whereas M5+ has a 5.5-inch display with 2500 mAh battery with other features similar to the M5.

### T series

T series from the company consists mid-range and high-end phones with T10 being the most expensive one. T10 has a unibody metal design with a 5.7 ” FULL HD LCD display. The phone is powered by MediaTek’s MT6797(Helio X20) 2.3 GHz deca-core processor with 4GB RAM and 32 GB internal storage(expandable up to 128 GB). Camera wise, the handset sports a 13 MP rear sensor, while selfies are taken care by an impressive 13 MP front camera. The phone runs on Android 6.0 with LEAGOO OS 2.1 skinned on top and has a 3500 mAh battery. The phone is currently priced at Rs. 49,999 in Nepal.

* **T1**  —*5.0-inch HD IPS 2.5D Display, 2GB RAM, 16GB ROM (128GB Expandable), 1.3GHz Quad Core Processor, Rear Camera 13mp w/ Dual Flash + 8mp front, Fingerprint scanner, 2400mAh Battery (Rs.****19,899)***
* **T1+** — *5.5-inch HD IPS 2.5D Display, 3GB RAM,16GB ROM (128GB Expandable), 1.3GHz Quad Core Processor, Camera-Rear 13mp/Dual Flash + Front 13mp AF, Fingerprint scanner, 2660mAh Li-Polymer Battery (Rs. **24,999**)*

With growing acceptance of Chinese brands around the world and in Nepal, LEAGOO smartphones can be a viable option for users willing to try a new brand for some extra features in low price. Though, we are yet to see how the brand performs in Nepali market with established competitors like Samsung and Huawei reigning current market shares.

title: Google’s PhotoScan App: Capture old photos digitally
content:
Before the smartphone revolution, everyone of us had our photos taken and printed. Though, all the images nowadays are either saved on social media or in the cloud. So, to digitize these old printed photos, Google has introduced PhotoScan app which allows everyone to take old photos and save them digitally along with a few updates to Google Photos app.

**Related in News:**  
[Huawei unveils the Mate 9 Lite with 4GB RAM and dual camera](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/11/huawei-unveils-mate-9-lite/) | [OnePlus 3T goes official with 6GB of RAM and 16MP front camera](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/11/oneplus-3t-goes-official/)

According to Google:

> PhotoScan is a new scanner app from Google Photos that lets you scan and save your favourite printed photos using your phone’s camera.

You can download it from the link below.

**[PhotoApp](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.google.android.apps.photos.scanner)**

Google is also rolling out a new update to the Google Photos app adding some new image editing features. The app will now come with 12 new image filters do be used with the photos. There is also a new option of auto enhancement which adjusts the exposure and saturation of the photos.

There is also ‘advanced’ editing tools by Google which allows users to tune the photos, including highlights, shadows, and warmth.

title: Xiaomi launches Redmi 4A & Redmi 4 : Specification and Price
content:
Xiaomi has finally added a new player to its budget phone market with its new Redmi 4 phones. After the recent surprise launch of [Mi Mix](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/10/xiaomi-mi-mix-unveiled-bezel-free-smartphone/) and long awaited [Mi Note 2](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/10/xiaomi-mi-note-2-officially-unveiled/), Xiaomi has finally updated the Redmi series with two phones: Redmi 4 and Redmi 4A.  The phones come with 5″ screens and Android 6.0 Marshmallow with varying specs.

**Related in News:**  
[Samsung Galaxy J1 Nxt Prime officially launched in Nepal](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/11/samsung-galaxy-j1-nxt-prime-price-nepal/) | [Intex launches three new Aqua phones in Nepal](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/11/intex-launches-three-new-aqua-phones-nepal/)

The most affordable phone, here, is the Redmi 4A with a price of CNY 499 ($74 USD). An all-metal body houses the Redmi 4A with a 5″ 720p LCD screen. The phone is powered by Snapdragon 425 chipset with Adreno 308 GPU, 2GB of RAM and 16 GB expandable internal storage.The phone has a 3,120 mAh battery and MIUI 8 skin over Android Marshmallow on the software side. There’s a 13 MP camera on the back and a 5MP camera on the back but lacks a fingerprint scanner.

Redmi 4 comes in two different editions — Standard and Pro edition. The first one comes with the same metal body and 720p display as the Redmi 4A but with 2.5D glass. It has a better Snapdragon 430 chipset with Adreno 505 GPU, and the same 2 GB RAM and 16 GB expandable storage. Redmi 4 sports a 13MP primary camera with phase detection autofocus and dual-LED flash along with the same 5MP front snapper. Unlike, Redmi 4A, this phone comes with a fingerprint sensor on the back and a huge 4,100 mAh battery. Redmi 4 Standard Edition is available in dark gray, gold, and rose gold for CNY 699 ($104 USD). The phones will be on sale from November 7th in China.

Although it has the same metal body, Redmi 4 Pro has much better features than the other two phones. It comes with a 5″ 1080p display and is powered by Snapdragon 625 chipset with Adreno 506 GPU, It also has increased RAM to 3GB and a larger 32 GB expandable storage. The phone comes with the same 13 MP primary camera but with dual-LED flash. Everything else is the same as  the Standard edition. It has a 5MP front camera, 4100 mAh battery and MIUI 8. The Redmi 4 Pro edition is priced at CNY 899 ($133 USD) and will be available to purchase on November 7th.

In the context of Nepal, Mi Nepal has previously sold Redmi 3 series in Nepal and it’s most likely that the Redmi 4 will also be on sale soon. Though, as usual, the phones will probably get a price bump if we have to guess based on the previous pricing footprints of Xiaomi phones in Nepal.

title: “Hello Again”: October 27 Apple event rumors and expectations
content:
Apple is all set to launch a redesigned MacBook Pro and some major Mac updates at the dedicated event tomorrow. Following its iPhone release back in September, Apple is probably revamping Mac series with a redesigned MacBook Pro and additional iMac and MacBook Air updates.

**Related in News:**  
[Apex Digital introduces its mobile chain store in Chitwan](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/10/apex-digital-chitwan/) | [Microsoft presents its Surface Book i7 and Surface Studio](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/10/microsoft-presents-surface-book-i7-surface-studio/)

### MacBook Pro

Talking about the MacBook Pro, which by the way desperately needs an upgrade will be facing a complete overhaul this time. Let’s take a look at some strong rumors.

**An OLED touch panel**: MacBook Pro will come with an OLED touch panel instead of function keys. The panel will allow users to change brightness, control volume, and music. It will also have a small section with useful touch options for any applications being used.

**Fingerprint Scanner:** The new MacBook Pro is also rumored to get a Touch ID fingerprint reader as revealed in some leaked photos. The reader is just beside the touch panel on the right.

**USB-C:** MacBook Pro is going to have USB-C ports, 4 to be exact according to the leaked picture.

If this is the case, there will be no power connector, HDMI port or even an SD card slot.

**Thinner and lighter?:** Of course, the new MacBook Pro is said to be thinner and lighter than the previous versions and will feature an improved flatter keyboard, mostly believed to be based on the Butterfly mechanism(like the new MacBook).

Rumors suggest the MacBook Pro will be available in similar resolutions as current models (2560 x 1600 for the 13-inch model and 2880 x 1800 for the 15-inch one), perhaps with better display technology and energy efficiency. Plus, the MacBook Pro is supposed to adopt Intel Skylake Processors and is rumored to come with 2TB SSD storage option.

### Also..

Other Mac products like iMac and MacBook Air are also getting updates tomorrow. The iMac is expected to get a 4k and 5k display options with new AMD graphics options. Rumors circulating the web suggests that MacBook Air will be graced with USB-C ports and some faster processors. And do you remember the AirPods introduced at the iPhone event? Apple should also be providing a release date for its wireless earbuds at the event. All these rumors are yet to be confirmed but we are pumped up to see what Apple has for its loyal customers.

The [event will be streaming live](http://www.apple.com/apple-events/october-2016/) at 1PM ET / 10:45 PM in Nepali time.

What are your expectations? Please do share with us in the comments.

title: Xiaomi Mi Mix unveiled: A bezel-free smartphone
content:
Alongside the launch of [Mi Note 2](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/10/xiaomi-mi-note-2-officially-unveiled/), Xiaomi managed to surprise techies all over the world by introducing a ‘concept’ phone featuring an almost bezel-less display. The phone comes with flagship specs under the hood and boasts a 6.4-inch edge-to-edge 1080p LCD display, though the bezel-free design should make its size similar to regular phablets. The phone features a full ceramic body with a glossy feel and has some major design modifications.

**Related in News:**  
[Microsoft presents its Surface Book i7 and Surface Studio](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/10/microsoft-presents-surface-book-i7-surface-studio/) | [Ncell brings Mobile TV pack – Watch online TV from your smartphone](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/10/ncell-mobile-tv-pack/)

It has no earpiece or a proximity sensor on the top and the front camera is dropped down. Xiaomi has replaced the earpiece speaker with a piezoelectric actuator behind the screen. The phone also uses ultrasound for proximity sensor, a feature used in a smartphone for the first time.

Apart from the screen, the Mi Mix hosts a 2.35 GHz Qualcomm Snapdragon 821 chipset with 6GB of RAM and 256GB of onboard storage. The phone has a 16MP camera with a fingerprint reader on the back and a 5MP selfie camera. The phone packs an impressive 4400 mAh battery with Quick Charge 3.0 support, which should last awhile given the screen resolution. Software wise the phone runs on Android 6.0 Marshmallow on top of MIUI 8.0.

Xiaomi Mi MIX will be available in China from November 4th and cost 3,499 yuan (around $516). The phone offers high-end specs and numerous design modifications. Being a concept phone, the Mi Mix might not be available in Nepal anytime soon, though, it remains to be seen how the phone performs in the Chinese market.

title: Kodak Ektra: Smartphone meant for photographers?
content:
Kodak, a company popular for its cameras back in the days have entered the smartphone market with its new smartphone, Kodak Ektra, named after a camera of the 1940s called Kodak [Ektra](http://camera-wiki.org/wiki/Kodak_Ektra) . The phone has a leather back with metal sides and a shutter button which gives it a camera like design. Now about the camera, the camera has large lenses on the back, something we have previously seen in the likes of Nokia Lumia 1020. But, it is only a 21 MP Sony lens opposed to the Lumia’s 41 MP camera.

**Related in News:**  
[CG unveils EON Blaze 6.0 and EON Blaze Pro smartphones in Nepal](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/10/cg-eon-blaze-6-0-eon-blaze-pro/) | [Nintendo Switch console unveiled, to go on sale from March 2017](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/10/nintendo-switch-console/)

### Camera Details

The Kodak Ektra features a standard smartphone camera with PDAF(Phase Detection AutoFocus), OIS, f/2.0 lens with HDR and 4k video support. On the front, Kodak packs a 13 MP camera with f/2.2 lens. The phone still offers some new perks in software side though with it’s own camera app featuring a *Scene Selection Tool*(a DSLR-like interface) with scene options like smart auto mode, manual, portrait, sports, bokeh, night, HDR, macro, landscape, and panorama. The manual mode allows adjusting exposure, ISO, focus, white balance and shutter speed — similar to SLRs. The camera app lets you edit photos with Snapseed, a photo editor app by Google, for instant editing options. Then, there is Super 8,  a video app which has vintage effects to add while recording videos.

### General Specification

Apart from the camera, Ektra comes with a MediaTek Helio X-20 SoC and a Mali-T880 GPU—3GB of RAM, 32GB of storage with microSD expansion. It has a 5.0 inch Full HD display and comes with Android 6.0. So, the phone almost is a standard smartphone with a camera like the design and a big lens. The camera doesn’t look as good on the paper as we have seen in other devices, but it still can outperform many smartphone cameras in the market.

The phone will sell in Europe later this year for €499 or £449, depending on the country.

title: ZTE Blade V7 Lite Review – A well built budget smartphone
content:
ZTE has entered the Nepali market with some appealing products for the general Nepali consumers. The budget range of phones with moderate feature set has made the ZTE phones a viable option for those seeking a good phone within an affordable price.

**Related in Reviews:**  
[Colors Pride P85+ Review – The best camera on a budget phone](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/12/colors-pride-p85-review-nepal/) | [Reviews](https://www.enepsters.com/category/reviews/)

The latest addition to this mobile series is the ZTE Blade V7 Lite which expects to satisfy smartphone users with its collection of noticeable specs and relatively low price of NRs. 18,990. However, is it really worth the price? Let’s take a look at the review and help you decide about it.

### Desgin and Build

The phone comes with a sleek design with almost no bezel at the side of the screens but with noticeably thick black borders around the screen which almost seems like a bezel. The phone still has a good look with its clever design trick. It has a metallic frame with a unibody-like design(but not completely unibody design as advertised) and a slightly thin metal back. The metal frame gives it a sturdy feel and the phone is light and comfortable in hand. The phone, therefore, has a good design and average build which is as much as we can get at this price.

### Display

The V7 Lite comes with a 5.0-inch 720p HD (720 x 1280 pixels) IPS LCD display with a pixel density of 294ppi and 2.5D curved glass on the front giving it a mixture of protection and aesthetic bonus with light curves at the edges. The display doesn’t do well under direct sunlight but handles day-to-day use with ease showing decent colors and average viewing angles.

### Software

Talking about the software, this phone comes with a slightly better Android v6.0 Marshmallow with ZTE’s Mi Favor UI skin on top by default. V7 Lite gives an android experience close to stock but with some of its own additions. The phone has a customized icon set, gestures and quick settings with some options to customize capacitive buttons from the settings menu. There is no app drawer in Mi Favor UI as expected from a Chinese manufacturer so all the apps are present on the home screen. If this is a problem for some, it can be easily solved by using an Android launcher.  We recommend Nova Launcher for this purpose but any decent launcher on the play store will do.

### Performance

Inside, the Blade V7 Lite is powered by MediaTek MT6735P quad-core chipset with a clock speed of 1.0GHz and Mali-T720MP2 GPU. The phone handles multitasking and day to day usage pretty darn good, thanks to the 2GB RAM. The phone has a 16GB internal memory which can be supplemented by adding microSD card of up to 256 GB as external memory. The phone makes for a good mobile experience with smooth calls and media consumption while handling HD videos with no noticeable lag. As for gaming, the phone struggles at some high demanding areas and also exhibits minor heating issues but does score a respectable score in our benchmark test.

### Camera

The phone sports a 13 MP primary camera on the back with LED flash. The phone’s sensor provides satisfactory images and when focused properly under suitable lighting conditions also clicks the picture with good details. However, the autofocus is not so good due to lack of some viable technology such as PDAF. The camera suffers in low-lighting conditions with dark pictures with image noise and reduced sharpness. Although this is one of those compromises we have to make at this price range.

The default camera app has an auto mode and a pro mode. The pro mode is like the manual mode in cameras with adjustable shutter speed and ISO. Advanced camera users, therefore, can tweak the camera’s settings to capture shots with preferred adjustments.

Here’s some camera sample clicked by us using the phone.

The front facing 8 MP camera takes good selfies with better details than other smartphones with 5 MP front cameras in its price range. The LED flash at the front also enables night time selfies and is ideal for low light conditions. Both cameras can record videos at 720P and have good video recording options.

### Battery

The Blade V7 Lite houses a 2500 mAh non-removable battery which can last about 4-4.5 hours for regular multimedia consumption and basic web browsing. For heavy users using the phone with maximum brightness and continuous high-end gaming, the battery can deplete more rapidly. Other than that, the battery should be enough for regular users and last a day of average use.

### Nice Feature Addition

The phone comes with a nice new addition which most of us really wanted in the latest budget phones, a *fingerprint reader*. Yes, the phone comes with a fingerprint reader and it can be used to unlock phone easily and also perform more actions configurable in the settings. By default, the fingerprint reader only works when the screen is on but it can be configured so as to unlock the phone even when the screen is off. The fingerprint is not as fast as one would want but it still performs well and provides slightly late but a smooth experience.

### Verdict

ZTE Blade v7 Lite, thus, provides most of the things you would expect from a smartphone at this price and some more. The phone is an excellent alternative to people who are tight on their budget but prefer excellent design and hardware. The phone easily challenges the budget market with new feature additions and has even outmatched its competitors at certain points. For a price of around 18,000, this phone provides an average 13 MP camera with a good 8 MP selfie snapper, fingerprint reader, and a clever design which appeals to most of the user base.

title: Xiaomi Redmi Note 4 pre-order starts from today, launching next week in Nepal
content:

title: ZTE Blade V7 Lite First Impressions – Fingerprint scanner on budget smartphone
content:
Budget smartphones are getting more features than ever, better cameras with greater megapixels and HD displays are the norms these days for any smartphone brands. The latest addition to these myriads of features is fingerprint scanner which packs an easier security option for the users.

**Related in Buying Guide:**  
[iPhone 7 & 7 Plus Price in Nepal](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/10/iphone-7-7-plus-price-nepal/) | [Is it worth purchasing smartphones from retailers like M.K. Trade Line?](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/08/m-k-trade-line/)

The official distributors for ZTE Smartphones, Evolution Enterprises, has introduced **ZTE Blade V7 Lite** with a price tag under Rs. 20,000 to provide a budget smartphone with a fingerprint scanner, plus other features which can compete with the phones of its price range. ZTE Blade V7 Lite has a sleek design with a metal frame body and an 8MP front face shooter with LED flash. On top of that, it also offers 4G support which makes this smartphone a sweet deal right now.

For now, the V7 Lite is priced at Rs. 18,990 with cash back offer of Rs. 700 as a special Dashain Offer along with a 10,000mAh powerbank.

### Design & Display

Looking at V7 Lite is kinda pleasing to the eye with its symmetric design and metal body build. It sports a 5.0-inch HD display with 294 ppi and a *curved* edge glass producing average colors and viewing angles. There are no side bezels on the phone on first look with thick black borders on screen. The curved edges of the glass and almost no side bezel like gives this phone pleasing aesthetics.

### Camera

Blade V7 Lite comes with a combination of 13MP rear camera(with autofocus) and 8MP front shooters with LED flash, both proving to be an excellent shooter for vibrant pictures and selfies. The rear camera performs arguably well in good lighting condition but tends to suffer in low light areas which are kind of a compromise at this price range for any phone. All in all the camera seems pretty decent for now.

### Performance and Battery

ZTE Blade V7 Lite hosts a Mediatek MT6735P Quad-Core chipset with Cortex-A53 processor combined with 2GB of RAM. The phone can handle general day to day activities with ease and does support multi-tasking without any performance lags. The phone has a MiFavor UI layered on top of Android 6.0 Marshmallow and has a customized icon set with no app drawer. Other than that the Android experience is similar to stock. Talking about the battery, V7 Lite packs a 2500mAh battery which can easily last a day with mild phone usage and media consumption.

### Conclusion

ZTE Blade V7 Lite provides a noteworthy set of features  with a good looking design, camera specs and a fingerprint scanner on the back . The phone is an excellent companion for users seeking exceptional features in the market and packs a lot of promise, especially as a budget friendly phone.

*Follow us for more details of this phone as we bring a more detailed review of this phone.*

title: Allocacoc Nepal brings Dashain offer
content:
Sammy Traders – the official distributor of Allocacoc products in Nepal has brought the Dashain offer. Dubbed as “Dashain Dhamaka” the offer consists exciting combo deals and discount on the purchase of Allocacoc products.
