title: Sastodeal launches its Android app: Shopping in Nepal made easier
content:
Sastodeal has launched its app on the Play Store. The online shopping platform has finally entered the play store and has an elegant application that makes shopping in Nepali market native to the Android platform. I was pretty much surprised when I used the app for the first time. The app hosts notable features you would want in your shopping app with a sidebar for navigation through your product categories.

**Related in News:**  
[Meizu M3 Max unveiled with 4,100mAh battery](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/09/meizu-m3-max-unveiled-4100mah-battery/) | [TP-Link unveils Neffos X1 and Neffos X1 Max smartphones](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/09/tp-link-unveils-neffos-x1-x1-max/)

It also has a product recommendation system displaying popular products among its users in the ‘*On Demand*‘ section. You can call it a Trending system same as in YouTube but for shopping items. You can also surf through the available products in the ‘*Discover*‘ section.

As a user, you can either set up an account at Sastodeal or log in with Facebook or Google account. Setting up an account can help to track your orders, purchase history and save items in your wishlist for future purchases.

There’s also a basic filter and sorting options to pick the product of preferred budget. You can also sort these lists of products in the price range and date of arrival in the market. Moreover, there are dedicated product pages sure make it a lot easier to shop and select items.

Unlike the mobile website, the app is much easier to use & shop in but has the functionalities of the official website. The program has a sleek materialistic design with swift animations and gives a smooth mobile experience for shopping. Kudos Sastodeal. You can download the app from this Google Play [Link](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.sastodeal.android), while iOS users have to wait for some duration since it’s currently in the development phase but as per them, the app will hit the App Store within coming week.

title: Great Festival Deals from Colors Mobile – Free Powerbank
content:
Colors Mobile Nepal has announced smart deals on their smartphones with a discounted price and a free 10,000mAh powerbank on some of its smartphones from this year – Colors E15, Colors E20, and Colors K3.

**Related in News:**  
[InFocus M370i and M808i unveiled in Nepal](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/08/infocus-m808i-nepal/) | [CAN CommTech to kick off from 23rd of Bhadra](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/08/can-com-tech-kick-off-23rd-bhadra/)

As stated by Raj Jung Thapa – Marketing Manager of Colors Mobile Nepal, “the whole idea behind giving away our best line-up smartphones on such a heavy price drop is to celebrate the start of the festival this year. The feel of the ownership of smartphones that too in such a heavy price drop is the smartest deal we can offer, along with a POWERBANK free”.

### Colors Elite E15

Colors Elite E15 packs a humongous 5,000 mAh battery with 59 days standby time and costs Rs. ~~13,995~~ 12,495 in Nepal. As far as the specs are concerned, the phone features 5-inch IPS LCD display with a resolution of 1280 x 720 pixels. Inside, the smartphone is baked with Mediatek MT6580 quad-core chipset clocked at 1.3GHz. The phone is coupled up with 2GB of RAM and 16GB of internal storage. In case you are wondering, the Colors Elite E15 sports an 8MP primary camera with an aperture rate of f/2.0 and LED flash. Upfront there is a 5MP sensor for selfies. With the combination of suitable specs, price tag, and 6 months replacement warranty the Colors Elite E15 is the budget phone to be desired.

### Colors Elite E20

Coming to the second phone the Color Elite E20 is from the same E-series but boasts a bigger 5.5-inch display and a better rear(13 MP) and front(8 MP) cameras than its smaller counterpart. Specs wise, the Colors Elite E20 features MediaTek MT6735 quad-core chipset bearing a clock speed of 1.2GHz. The Elite E20 is integrated with 2GB of RAM and 16GB of internal storage, which is similar to its counterpart but unfortunately compromises in its battery capacity  – featuring just a 2500mAh battery. Despite its less powerful battery Colors Elite E20, still is a good phone, especially looking at the price it comes with. At Rs. 13,995 instead of the original 15,595 price tag, the phone brings a comparatively powerful specs in a comfortable price tag.

### Colors K3

Talking about the third and the premium one the Colors K3 noticeably features 3GB of RAM and 32GB of internal storage for heavy users and gamers with a classy design and eye-popping pearl black color. The Colors K3 sports a 5-inch HD (1280 x 720) IPS display protected with Asahi Dragontrail Glass both on the front and back of the phone which will prove to be helpful for preventing the phone against scratches. Inside, the smartphone is powered by MediaTek MT6735 64-bit quad-core chipset with a clock speed of 1.3GHz. Talking about the camera, the Colors K3 features 13MP CMOS rear camera with f/2.2 aperture rate and LED flash accompanied by an 8MP CMOS camera up front for selfies. Other noticeable feature includes a support for dual-SIM with 4G capability which is significantly missing from both phone(mentioned above).  Colors K3 is originally priced at Rs. 17,495 but will cost you Rs. 15,995 with a free 10,000mAh powerbank during the offer period.

The above-mentioned devices are the best phones you can get from Colors Mobile and in case you are wandering around for great deals on smartphones you better hurry up as the deals are **limited** until the start of the festivals of this year. These smart deals are worth considering for buying some decent phone for great prices. The deals will be valid across Nepal and can be redeemed from any authorized retailers of Colors Mobile located near you.

title: Huawei GR5 Mini Price in Nepal
content:
Huawei Mobile Nepal has officially launched the GR5 Mini in Nepal for the price of Rs. 23,500. The GR5 Mini is the miniature version of [Huawei GR5](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/02/huawei-gr5-price-in-nepal/), which was launched a year ago in Nepal.

**Related in News:**  
[Huawei Y6 II officially launched in Nepal with 5.5-inch display](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/08/huawei-y6-ii-nepal/) | [LeEco Le 2s to feature 8GB of RAM?](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/08/leeco-le-2s-feature-8gb-ram/)

The Huawei GR5 Mini features a 5.2-inch 1080p display which is comparatively smaller to GR5’s 5.5-inch. Inside, the smartphone is powered by Huawei’s in-house Kirin 650 octa-core chipset bearing a respectable clock speed of 2GHz Cortex-A53 as well as 1.7GHz Cortex-A53 CPU. Even though the GR5 Mini is a miniature version of Huawei GR5, the smartphone tend to feature upgraded and powerful chipset. Alongside, the new Kirin 650 SoC, the GR5 Mini is also baked with an interactive and intelligent i5 co-processor, which as claimed by Huawei will be helpful for day to day activities like sensor hub, speech recognition, Fused Location Provider navigation, alongside more effective and efficient location-based system. The Huawei GR5 is coupled up with 2GB of RAM and 16GB of internal storage which can be expanded via a microSD card up to 128GB.

As far as the camera is concerned, the Huawei GR5 Mini sports a 13MP primary sensor with auto-focus and LED flash accompanied by an 8MP selfie shooter up front. In case you are wondering, the phone also features a v2.0 fingerprint scanner which is believed to unlock the phone in mere 0.5 seconds. The fingerprint is not limited to phone’s security only as you can use the sensor to take selfies and answer phone calls too.

Connectivity options of the phone include a support for dual-SIM with 4G capability, WiFi b/g/n, Bluetooth 4.1, and a GPS. The whole phone runs on Android 6.0 Marshmallow on top of Huawei’s EMUI 4.1. The whole phone is powered by a Lithium Polymer (Li-Po) 3000mAh battery.

Huawei GR5 Mini packs all the necessary features and specs which make it an appropriate choice for users who are seeking a proper alternatives to flagship smartphones. Alongside those impressive specs, the brushed metal body of the GR5 mini will definitely not let you feel the cheapness of this smartphone. Although the phone is a miniature version, the GR5 Mini proves to be a proper successor to its well-known brother – GR5.

title: Best online learning resources – Free
content:
To better understand a topic we skim through books, articles and ask others which is alright for a rudimentary understanding of the topic but for more detailed knowledge of the topic or to learn a new skill, we usually take  classes in universities. The new learners can take the online courses without any commitments and can learn many things via the structured curriculum each course provides. Here we have listed some major MOOC (Massive Online Open Courses) sites to use for online learning and boosting your resume with additional skills.

**Related in Best Of Us:**  
[5 Android apps you should consider deleting right now](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/08/5-android-apps-you-should-delete-now/) | [Obvious reasons to delete your Facebook account](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/05/obvious-reasons-delete-facebook-account/)

### [edX](http://edx.org)

[edX](http://edx.org) is a massive open online course provider which hosts online university-level courses in a wide range of disciplines to a worldwide student body, including some courses at no charge. Most of the courses are free to take and there are a lot of categories of study to browse through. It is open and many initiatives like MITx has followed with edX.

### [Coursera](http://www.coursera.org)

[Coursera](http://www.coursera.org) is MOOC site with a wide range of courses to choose from. It has a pretty impressive list of courses and also allows certificate for a fee after completion of courses. Both Coursera and edX provide courses in video form through quizzes and assignment submission methods vary. Coursera has a peer review process for assignments and has online quizzes for assessing the students.

### [Khan Academy](http://www.khanacademy.org)

[Khan Academy](http://www.khanacademy.org), founded by Salman Khan(not the actor), hosts self-prepared courses for learners instead of university courses. It follows a more interactive approach to studying the subject matter and harness the full power of online learning.

### Bonus

Here’re some bonus materials for you to skim through.

[iTunes U Free Courses](https://itunes.apple.com/us/app/itunes-u/id490217893?mt=8) : It hosts a list of courses from several universities accessible through iTune.

[Math for fun and glory](https://www.khanacademy.org/math/math-for-fun-and-glory) : A collection of mathematical explanations on Khan Academy.

[Learning How to Learn](https://www.coursera.org/learn/learning-how-to-learn) : Course on learning techniques on Coursera.

[cs50](https://www.edx.org/course/introduction-computer-science-harvardx-cs50x) : A Computer Science course on edX.

title: Micromax Canvas 6 launched in Nepal for the price of Rs. 23,650
content:
Pashupati Trade Link – Micromax’s official distributor in Nepal has officially launched its flagship phone of the year entitled as Canvas 6 for the price of Rs. 23,650. The major highlights of this smartphone are its metallic body and a fingerprint sensor at the back of the phone.

**Related in News:**  
[Micromax Canvas Selfie 4 launched in Nepal for the price of Rs.11,110](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/07/micromax-canvas-selfie-4-8mp-front-camera-launched-nepal/) | [Users can now play football in Facebook Messenger](https://www.enepsters.com/2016/06/users-can-now-play-football-messenger/)

The Canvas 6 sports a 5.5-inch full-HD display resulting a resolution of 1080 x 1920 pixel with a pixel density of 401 ppi protected with Corning Gorilla Glass 3. Inside, the smartphone is powered by MediaTek’s 2.0GHz octa-core MT6753 Helio X10 processor coupled up with 3GB of RAM and 32GB of expandable storage.

Taking a look at the camera, the Canvas 6 features an average 13MP primary camera at the back with big black hump like Nexus 6P with an aperture rate of f/2.0 and LED flash. The phone sports an 8MP front facing camera for decent selfies. The whole phone runs on Android 5.1 Lollipop and is powered by an average 3,000mAh battery.

Connectivity wise, the Canvas 6 is 4G enabled and supports dual-SIM alongside other options like WiFi a/b/g/n/ac, Bluetooth 4.1, FM and GPS. The phone is integrated with sensors like Proximity sensor, Ambient light sensor and Accelerometer but lacks Gyroscopic sensor.

With more people opting in for Chinese brands like Xiaomi and Huawei in Nepal, Micromax has gone missing from the smartphone market for quite a while. Considering the price tag, the Canvas 6 is right choice for buyers seeking an affordable smartphone with impressive specs, as well as might be the right direction for Micromax to get in track and compete head to head against the likes of other Chinese brands in the market. The Canvas 6 can be purchased from authorized retailers across Nepal.

[*Know the full specs of Canvas 6 here.*](https://www.enepsters.com/product/micromax-canvas-6/)

title: Huawei launches Honor 5A with 13MP camera
